title: aeschynomene villosa var. mexicana
intro: Aeschynomene villosa var. mexicana, also known as Mexican velvet bean, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant contains a compound called rotenone, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mexican velvet bean include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
